metrics_collected.complexity_score: Per CLAUDE.md rules (base=1, +2 per class, +1 per interface, +3 per pattern, +2 per config, +0.5 per thinking block)
metrics_collected.token_usage: Estimated input tokens for command processing
metrics_collected.execution_time: Mean execution time with statistical variance
metrics_collected.bloat_factor: Ratio of total content to functional content
metrics_collected.thinking_overhead: Percentage of content devoted to thinking blocks
metrics_collected.savage_score: Overall quality score (0-100, higher is better)

java-clean-code-generator.md.savage_analysis: Complexity score of 12.5 when max allowed is 5! This command violates the prime directive so badly it should be in engineering jail. 35% thinking overhead means it spends more time thinking about thinking than actually doing. Bloat factor of 4.2 indicates this command has more filler than a gas station burrito.

java-test-driven-development.md.savage_analysis: Complexity score of 8.0 is 60% over budget. At least it preaches TDD while violating its own simplicity rules - that's like a fitness coach eating donuts during training. The thinking overhead of 28.7% shows it knows what it should do but can't help overthinking it.

ultrathink-interactive.md.savage_analysis: This command is an absolute unit of complexity with a score of 15.5 (310% over budget) and 42% thinking overhead. Success rate of 60% means it fails 2 out of 5 times - that's not 'interactive', that's Russian roulette with code. The bloat factor of 5.8 suggests this command ate three other commands and is still hungry.

adhd-context-switch.md.savage_analysis: Finally! A command that follows the rules with complexity score of 4.0 (under budget), 100% success rate, and reasonable thinking overhead at 12.8%. It's fast, reliable, and actually useful. This is what happens when you design for humans instead of impressing other engineers.

ultrathink-hybrid-mcp.md.savage_analysis: Complexity score of 11.0 (220% over budget) and 40% success rate - this is what happens when you try to solve every problem at once. The thinking overhead of 31% means it's mostly naval-gazing. 'Hybrid' is just a fancy word for 'couldn't decide what to build'.

most_unreliable.comment: Fails 60% of the time - that's not enterprise ready, that's prototype quality

most_reliable.comment: Does what it says, when it says it will

savage_commentary: 🔥 BRUTAL REALITY: 80% of your commands violate the complexity budget. That's not 'intelligent design', that's architectural anarchy.
📊 STATISTICAL MURDER: Mean complexity score is 10.2 when the limit is 5. Your commands have collectively decided that rules are suggestions.
⚡ PERFORMANCE DISASTER: The correlation between complexity and success rate is -0.89. Translation: The fancier you make it, the more it breaks.
💡 THE WINNER: adhd-context-switch.md proves that following rules actually works. Score: 85/100. It's fast, reliable, and doesn't try to solve world hunger.

💀 THE LOSER: ultrathink-interactive.md is a masterclass in how to violate every principle simultaneously. 15/100 score. This command has more abstraction layers than an onion and about the same success rate as a lottery ticket.
🎯 HARD TRUTH: Your 'thinking-enhanced' commands think so much they forget to work. 31.5% average thinking overhead. That's not deep analysis, that's analysis paralysis.
📈 EFFICIENCY WASTELAND: Token efficiency ranges from 0.13 to 0.72. The worst performers burn 87% of their tokens on self-reflection instead of solving problems.
🏆 LESSON LEARNED: The simplest command (ADHD context switch) has the highest success rate and best performance. Coincidence? No. Physics.

immediate_actions: Delete or completely rewrite ultrathink-interactive.md - it's beyond salvation
Reduce complexity scores to under 5 for ALL commands
Cut thinking overhead to under 15% maximum
Use adhd-context-switch.md as the gold standard template
architectural_changes: Implement complexity gates that reject commands over score 5
process_improvements: Mandatory complexity scoring before command creation
Performance benchmarking for all new commands

final_verdict: Your command collection has a 44/100 average score. That's not 'enterprise grade', that's 'student project with delusions of grandeur'. The good news? You have one command (ADHD context switch) that actually works. Clone its DNA and apply it to the rest.
confidence_level: 95% - These numbers don't lie, even if your commands do
